rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxyselenation of carbamate- and benzoate-tethered terminal alkenes with diphenyl diselenide to deliver β-hydroxyselenides via an anodically generated selenium electrophile and intramolecular cyclic seleniranium intermediate opened by water.
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of PhSeSePh to a selenonium radical cation (A) which fragments to PhSe• (B) and PhSe+ (C); the cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: Six-membered seleniranium/cyclic selenonium intermediate (D) from intramolecular carbamate-O assistance

electron_flow: PhSeSePh is oxidized at the graphite anode to a radical cation that splits into PhSe• and PhSe+; the electrophilic PhSe+ adds to the tethered alkene with anchimeric assistance from the carbamate/ester oxygen to form a six-membered cyclic selenonium ion D, which is opened by water/hydroxide to give the β-hydroxyselenide while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a still formed in 65% yield, indicating the productive pathway is not radical.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a still obtained in 49% yield, supporting a non-radical (cationic) pathway.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclohexene (6)
probe_reagents.observation: No β-hydroxyselenide product 8 formed; non-tethered simple alkenes are unreactive, supporting the need for an intramolecular nucleophile to form intermediate D.

probe_reagents.name: Allylbenzene (7)
probe_reagents.observation: No desired product 9, again indicating tether-assisted seleniranium opening is required.

notes: Substrate-scope failures (cyano, nitro, dimethylcarbamate, cinnamyl) are rationalized by competing cathodic reduction of EWGs or anodic oxidation of N-methyl groups rather than by a change in mechanism. X-ray structure of 3j (CCDC 2408983) confirms the β-hydroxyselenide regiochemistry consistent with seleniranium-opening pathway. A radical chain involving PhSe• cannot be fully excluded but is disfavored by TEMPO/BHT results.
